era_context: Mid-8th century BCE: Tiglath-Pileser III's Assyrian Empire is in the midst of aggressive territorial expansion and administrative reform. The Zhou Dynasty in China remains fragmented. Greek civilization is in the Archaic period. Classic Maya civilization is flourishing in Mesoamerica.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of the Americas outside Mesoamerica
Greece and the Mediterranean (limited written records for this specific year)

events.title: Tiglath-Pileser III's Levantine Campaigns (Continued)
events.description: Tiglath-Pileser III continues his military campaigns in the Levant as part of his broader imperial expansion strategy. The campaigns involve conquest of local kingdoms and implementation of mass deportation policies. These actions establish Assyrian hegemony over the region.
events.certainty_note: Tiglath-Pileser III's reign (745–727 BCE) is well-documented, but specific year-by-year dating within his reign has uncertainty of ±2–3 years. This year is assigned by convention within the broader campaign period.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: 759 BCE is extremely poorly documented. The only detailed contemporary sources are Assyrian royal inscriptions. The historical record for this year is almost entirely dependent on Assyrian sources. Events in other regions are unrecorded or preserved only in much later sources. The sparse documentation means that significant events may have occurred without leaving traces in surviving sources. The historical record for this year is heavily skewed toward Assyrian military and administrative activities.
